summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting covering a wide range of topics including a cybersecurity policy adoption, a detailed presentation on the Sunnymead TIF district with a resolution to seek its elimination, a comprehensive strategic plan review, and several fire department personnel actions. The board also approved pursuing an OPWC grant for 2026 road paving in collaboration with the Clermont County Engineer, approved street lighting assessment adjustments, and scheduled a zoning workshop for September 8, 2025.

attendees: Director Eichenhorst (also referred to as Eichens/Eikenhorst)
notes: The meeting included a very detailed presentation by Administrator Rose on the Sunnymead TIF district, explaining how TIF financing works, that the township only receives approximately $2 per condo in taxes due to the frozen baseline, and that over $1 million of TIF funds went to the county's Traffic Improvement District with no projects done in Goshen Township. The strategic plan review covered the full 2020-2026 five-year plan showing significant accomplishments including $7 million in grants secured. Discussion of road assessment methodology for neighborhoods was notable - Engineer Evans acknowledged the township is in a 'tough spot' without a roads levy. The board discussed potential future use of TIFs, JEDs, and RIDs for economic development, particularly related to the Eagle's Nest/Dries Homes development project. Executive session lasted approximately one hour (7:03-8:08 p.m.) and resulted in four fire department personnel actions.
title: BOT 8-26-25
